LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. A R G U E D. THE COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON JANUARY 4, 2018. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A BRIEF RESPONSE TO THE ALVAREZ POINT ONLY AS TO WHY PLAINTIFF DISAGREES WITH THE CITY'S ANALYSIS WITH REGARD TO PRONG TWO. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A REVISED PROPOSED ORDER DENYING THE MOTION AS A WHOLE AND DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER GRANTING THE MOTION IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G). SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF AND PROPOSED ORDERS DUE BY 1/4/18. JUDGE: ANDREW Y.S. CHENG, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576, RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

Hearing On The Petition To Approve The Compromise Of A Disputed Claim Of The Minor, Peter Nguyen
Off Calendar Dec-06-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Pending Action of the Minor, Peter Nguyen.
Dept. 514. Judge: Eric R. Fleming. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Kimberly Wong, Esq. appeared with petitioner Quan Nguyen, who was present as Guardian ad Litem for the minor, Peter Nguyen, who was not present at this morning's hearing.
Petitioner was placed under oath and testified.
Order appointing Mr. Nguyen Guardian ad Litem for the minor was filed on July 12, 2016.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the compromise is approved as follows:

Settlement Amount - $100,000.00.
Attorney Fees - $25,000.00.
Medical Bills/Liens - $20,693.39.
Costs - $33,803.88.

The net balance of $20,502.73 shall be used for the purchase of an annuity from Metropolitan Tower Life Insurance Company, with guaranteed payouts as follows:

(A) $10,000.00 lump sum payable on 07/13/2027; and
(B) $16,830.85 lump sum payable on 07/13/2029.

Order Approving the Compromise of a Pending Action of the Minor was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Matter is CONTINUED to January 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for Progress Report on the purchase of the annuity from Metropolitan Tower Life Insurance Company.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-05-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Craig Peters, Esq. 415-673-4800 of The Veen Firm for Plaintiff.
James Hannawalt, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3913 of the Office of the City Attorney for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues 2 grounds, cites Alvarez and Hampton, has etablished all elements to design immunity and the Court erred on requiring personal knowledge. Plaintiff responds, the City has failed on three prongs. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is taken under submission on January 4, 2018. Plaintiff to submit a brief response to the Alvarez point only as to why Plaintiff disagrees with the City's analysis with regard to prong two. Plaintiff to submit a revised proposed order denying the motion as a whole and Defendant to submit a proposed order granting the motion in compliance with CCP 437c(g). Supplemental brief and proposed orders due by 1/4/18.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, rhunter@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/AYSC)
